---
title: "Kernel Estimator"
author:   
  - Jinzhe Yang
format: 
  html: default
  pdf:
    keep-tex: true
    include-in-header: 
       text: |
         \usepackage{fvextra}
         \DefineVerbatimEnvironment{Highlighting}{Verbatim}{breaklines,commandchars=\\\{\}}
         \DefineVerbatimEnvironment{OutputCode}{Verbatim}{breaklines,commandchars=\\\{\}}
toc: true
language: 
  title-block-author-single: " "
toc-title-document: "Content"
crossref-fig-title: "Figure"
theme: lumen
title-block-banner-color: Primary
callout-warning-caption: "Warning"
callout-note-caption: "Note!"
callout-tip-caption: "Tip"
---

## Product Kernel Estimator
$$
\hat f_{X,Y}(x,y)
=
\frac{1}{n h_x h_y}
\sum_{i=1}^{n}
K\!\left(\frac{x-X_i}{h_x}\right)
K\!\left(\frac{y-Y_i}{h_y}\right)
$$

```{r}

set.seed(123)

n <- 500
x <- runif(n, -3, 3)

# Heteroskedastic noise
sigma <- 0.3 + 0.2 * abs(x)
y <- sin(x) + rnorm(n, 0, sigma)

# Define kernel and bandwidths
K <- function(u) dnorm(u)

hx <- 0.4
hy <- 0.3

# Choose evaluation grid
x0 <- 1 # Fix X = 1
y_grid <- seq(-2, 2, length.out = 200)

# Compute conditional density at X = 1
cond_density <- sapply(y_grid, function(y0) {
    weights_x <- K((x0 - x) / hx)
    numerator <- sum(weights_x * K((y0 - y) / hy))
    denominator <- sum(weights_x)
    numerator / (hy * denominator)
})

# Plot Conditional Density at X = 1
plot(y_grid, cond_density,
    type = "l",
    lwd = 3,
    main = "Estimated Conditional Density at X = 1",
    xlab = "y",
    ylab = "f(y | X = 1)"
)

# To visualize the full conditional density surface:
x_grid <- seq(-3, 3, length.out = 50)
y_grid <- seq(-2, 2, length.out = 100)

density_matrix <- outer(x_grid, y_grid, Vectorize(function(x0, y0) {
    weights_x <- K((x0 - x) / hx)
    numerator <- sum(weights_x * K((y0 - y) / hy))
    denominator <- sum(weights_x)
    numerator / (hy * denominator)
}))

# Plot surface:
persp(x_grid, y_grid, density_matrix,
    theta = 30, phi = 30,
    xlab = "X",
    ylab = "Y",
    zlab = "f(Y|X)"
)
```

## Non-parametric Estimate Package
```{r}
library(np)

model_np <- npcdens(y ~ x)

summary(model_np)

newdata <- expand.grid(
    x = seq(-3, 3, length = 50),
    y = seq(-2, 2, length = 100)
)

pred <- predict(model_np, newdata = newdata)
# persp(newdata.x, newdata.y, pred,
#       theta = 30, phi = 30,
#       xlab = "X",
#       ylab = "Y",
#       zlab = "f(Y|X)")
# newdata[:, 'x']
# reshape predictions to matrix
z <- matrix(pred, nrow = 50, ncol = 100)

x_grid <- seq(-3, 3, length = 50)
y_grid <- seq(-2, 2, length = 100)

persp(x_grid, y_grid, z,
    theta = 30, phi = 30,
    xlab = "X",
    ylab = "Y",
    zlab = "f(Y|X)",
    main = "Conditional Density Surface"
)

image(x_grid, y_grid, z,
    xlab = "X",
    ylab = "Y",
    main = "Conditional Density f(Y|X)",
    col = terrain.colors(50)
)

contour(x_grid, y_grid, z,
    xlab = "X",
    ylab = "Y",
    main = "Conditional Density Contours"
)

image(x_grid, y_grid, z,
    col = heat.colors(50),
    xlab = "X",
    ylab = "Y",
    main = "Conditional Density"
)

contour(x_grid, y_grid, z, add = TRUE)
```

```{r}
x_grid <- seq(-2, 2, length = 1000)
plot(x_grid, dnorm(x_grid, 0, 0.1), type = "l")
```

## Multinormal Distribution
```{r}
# ==============================
# 1. Setup
# ==============================

set.seed(123)

# Mean vector
mu <- c(0, 0)

# Covariance matrix (strong positive correlation)
Sigma <- matrix(
    c(
        4, 3.2,
        3.2, 4
    ),
    nrow = 2
)

# ==============================
# 2. Simulate multivariate normal data
# ==============================

# install.packages("MASS")  # run once if needed
library(MASS)

n <- 1000
data <- mvrnorm(n, mu = mu, Sigma = Sigma)

# ==============================
# 3. Define two comparison points
# ==============================

point_A <- c(2, 2)
point_B <- c(2, -2)

# ==============================
# 4. Compute distances
# ==============================

# Euclidean distance
euclidean_A <- sqrt(sum((point_A - mu)^2))
euclidean_B <- sqrt(sum((point_B - mu)^2))

# Mahalanobis distance
mahal_A <- sqrt(mahalanobis(point_A, center = mu, cov = Sigma))
mahal_B <- sqrt(mahalanobis(point_B, center = mu, cov = Sigma))

# Print results
cat("Euclidean distance A:", euclidean_A, "\n")
cat("Euclidean distance B:", euclidean_B, "\n\n")

cat("Mahalanobis distance A:", mahal_A, "\n")
cat("Mahalanobis distance B:", mahal_B, "\n")

# ==============================
# 5. Plot data and tilted ellipse
# ==============================

# Basic scatter plot
plot(data,
    col = rgb(0, 0, 1, 0.3),
    pch = 16,
    xlab = "X1",
    ylab = "X2",
    main = "Tilted Multivariate Normal with Two Points"
)

# Add mean
points(mu[1], mu[2], pch = 19, col = "black", cex = 1.5)

# Add comparison points
points(point_A[1], point_A[2], col = "red", pch = 19, cex = 1.5)
points(point_B[1], point_B[2], col = "darkgreen", pch = 19, cex = 1.5)

# Add lines from mean to points
segments(mu[1], mu[2], point_A[1], point_A[2], col = "red", lwd = 2)
segments(mu[1], mu[2], point_B[1], point_B[2], col = "darkgreen", lwd = 2)

# ==============================
# 6. Add covariance ellipse
# ==============================

# install.packages("ellipse")  # run once if needed
library(ellipse)

ell <- ellipse(Sigma, centre = mu, level = 0.95)
lines(ell, col = "black", lwd = 2)

legend("topleft",
    legend = c(
        "Simulated data",
        "Mean",
        "Point A (2,2)",
        "Point B (2,-2)",
        "95% Ellipse"
    ),
    col = c(rgb(0, 0, 1, 0.3), "black", "red", "darkgreen", "black"),
    pch = c(16, 19, 19, 19, NA),
    lty = c(NA, NA, NA, NA, 1),
    lwd = c(NA, NA, NA, NA, 2)
)
```

```{r}
library(MASS) # for mvrnorm
library(betareg)

set.seed(123)

n <- 1000 # sample size
mu_beta <- c(0, 0, 0) # means of X1, X2, X3

# Covariance matrix for correlated covariates
Sigma <- matrix(c(
    1, 0.5, 0.3,
    0.5, 1, 0.4,
    0.3, 0.4, 1
), nrow = 3)

# Generate correlated covariates
X <- mvrnorm(n, mu = mu_beta, Sigma = Sigma)
X1 <- X[, 1]
X2 <- X[, 2]
X3 <- X[, 3]

# simulate response
phi <- 5 # precision parameter
beta <- c(0.5, 0.7, -0.3, 0.2) # beta0, beta1, beta2, beta3

# Linear predictor
eta <- beta[1] + beta[2] * X1 + beta[3] * X2 + beta[4] * X3

# Inverse logit to get mean in (0,1)
mu <- plogis(eta)

# Simulate beta response
Y <- rbeta(n, shape1 = mu * phi, shape2 = (1 - mu) * phi)


fit <- betareg(Y ~ X1 + X2 + X3, link = "logit")
summary(fit)

cor(X) # correlation matrix of covariates

# Combine response and covariates
sim_data <- data.frame(
    Y = Y,
    X1 = X1,
    X2 = X2,
    X3 = X3
)

# Quick look
head(sim_data)
write.csv(sim_data, "simulated_beta_data.csv", row.names = FALSE)


hist(Y, freq=FALSE)
min(Y)
max(Y)
```

```{r}

x_new <- data.frame(X1 = 0, X2 = 1, X3 = 2)

# Predict conditional mean
mu_hat <- predict(fit, newdata = x_new, type = "response")
mu_hat
```
